lesson.menu_title: Review & Real-Life Roleplays -- Doctor, Pharmacy, Getting Help
lesson.menu_desc: Final review: put it all together in real situations -- at the doctor (I have a headache. You should rest.), at the pharmacy (Can I have a painkiller, please? How much is it?), and getting help in the city (Excuse me, how do I get to...? Go straight.) -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Review & Real-Life Roleplays: Doctor, Pharmacy, Getting Help
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Érica Maria Machado Santarem | Aula 20 -- Review & Real-Life Roleplays

listenings.text: Hello. Let me tell you about a normal day. In the morning, I did not feel well. I had a headache and a fever. So I went to the doctor. The doctor said: You should rest and drink water. Then I went to the pharmacy. I said: Can I have a painkiller, please? The pharmacist asked: Do you have a prescription? I said: No, just a painkiller. I asked: How much is it? It was twelve reais. On the way home, I was lost. So I asked a man on the street: Excuse me, how do I get to Pinheiros station? He said: Go straight and turn left. Now I am home. I rested, and I feel much better.

Hello. Let me give you three simple tips for real life in English. Tip one: at the doctor, say how you feel. I have a headache. I have a fever. The doctor will say: You should rest. Tip two: at the pharmacy, ask politely. Can I have a painkiller, please? And to know the price, ask: How much is it? Tip three: when you are lost, ask for help. Excuse me, how do I get to the station? People will help you. Go straight, turn left, turn right. You can do all of this now. Be calm, speak slowly, and take your time.

extra_audio.text: First, I tell the doctor: I have a headache. Second, the doctor says: You should rest. Third, at the pharmacy I ask: Can I have a painkiller, please? Fourth, I ask: How much is it? Fifth, on the street I ask: Excuse me, how do I get to the station?
